item_a.name: Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed
item_a.image_alt: Sleep Number 360 i8 Smart Bed with adjustable air chambers and smart technology
pros: Adjustable firmness from 0-100 on each side
Responsive sleep tracking and biometric monitoring
Zero gravity preset and partner snore technology
Long-term durability with replaceable air chambers
cons: Higher maintenance with air pump components
Can feel less contouring than memory foam
item_a.best_for: Couples with different firmness preferences who want detailed sleep tracking and the ability to fine-tune their mattress over time.

item_b.name: Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid
item_b.image_alt: Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt mattress showing memory foam layers and hybrid construction
pros: Superior pressure relief with proprietary TEMPUR material
Excellent motion isolation for undisturbed sleep
No mechanical parts or electricity required
cons: Fixed firmness level cannot be adjusted
Retains more heat than air-based mattresses
Heavy weight (100-150 lbs) makes moving difficult
item_b.best_for: Individuals seeking exceptional pressure relief and body contouring who have already identified their preferred firmness level.

comparison_summary: Sleep Number offers customizable firmness with air chamber technology and advanced sleep tracking, while Tempur-Pedic delivers superior pressure relief through proprietary memory foam. Sleep Number wins for couples needing different firmness levels, but Tempur-Pedic provides better value and simpler ownership for those who know their ideal firmness.
verdict_text: Sleep Number 360 wins for adjustability-focused couples, while Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-Adapt excels for pressure relief seekers who prefer simplicity.

key_differences.item_a: 0-100 adjustable scale on each side via air chambers
key_differences.item_b: Fixed firmness (soft, medium, or firm models available)

key_differences.item_a: Good support but less contouring than foam
key_differences.item_b: Exceptional contouring with NASA-developed TEMPUR material

key_differences.item_a: Built-in SleepIQ technology with heart rate and breathing monitoring
Moderate - air can transfer some movement
key_differences.item_b: Excellent - memory foam absorbs nearly all motion

key_differences.item_a: Cooler sleeping with air circulation and DualTemp layer option
key_differences.item_b: Retains more heat, though newer models include cooling gel

key_differences.item_a: Requires electricity, potential air pump servicing

seo_content:
When comparing Sleep Number vs Tempur-Pedic, you're evaluating two fundamentally different approaches to premium sleep. Sleep Number built its reputation on adjustable air chamber technology that allows sleepers to dial in their perfect firmness from 0 to 100, while Tempur-Pedic pioneered memory foam mattresses with their proprietary TEMPUR material originally developed for NASA.

The Sleep Number 360 i8 represents the pinnacle of customizable sleep technology. Each side of the mattress can be adjusted independently, making it ideal for couples with vastly different firmness preferences. One partner might prefer a plush 35 setting while the other sleeps comfortably at a firm 75. The integrated SleepIQ technology tracks biometrics including heart rate variability, breathing rate, and movement throughout the night, providing detailed insights through a smartphone app. The responsive air system can even detect snoring and automatically adjust the head position to help reduce it. However, this technology comes at a premium price point of $3,999-$5,499 and requires electricity to function.

Tempur-Pedic vs Sleep Number presents a different value proposition for those prioritizing pressure relief and simplicity. The TEMPUR-Adapt Medium Hybrid combines the brand's signature memory foam with a supportive coil system, delivering exceptional body contouring that adapts to your unique shape. This material excels at relieving pressure points around shoulders, hips, and lower back—particularly beneficial for side sleepers and those with chronic pain. Motion isolation is superior to Sleep Number, making it the better choice when one partner tosses and turns. At $2,199-$3,799, Tempur-Pedic also offers better entry-level value, though you sacrifice the ability to change firmness later.

The Sleep Number advantage becomes clear for couples who can't agree on firmness or those who anticipate their preferences changing due to weight fluctuations, injuries, or aging. The technology allows you to adapt the mattress rather than replacing it. Conversely, Tempur-Pedic requires less maintenance with no mechanical components, runs silently without pumps or motors, and provides that classic memory foam hug that many sleepers prefer. Temperature regulation favors Sleep Number's air-based design, while Tempur-Pedic's denser foam retains more body heat despite cooling gel infusions in newer models. Your decision ultimately depends on whether you value adjustability and data (Sleep Number) or proven pressure relief and simplicity (Tempur-Pedic).

faq.q: Which lasts longer, Sleep Number or Tempur-Pedic?
faq.a: Both brands offer 10+ year lifespans, but Sleep Number's replaceable air chambers can extend usability beyond 15 years, while Tempur-Pedic's foam typically maintains support for 10-12 years before showing significant wear.

faq.q: Is Sleep Number better than Tempur-Pedic for back pain?
faq.a: Tempur-Pedic generally provides superior pressure relief for back pain due to its contouring properties, though Sleep Number's adjustability allows you to find your optimal firmness level which can be equally effective for some back pain sufferers.

faq.q: Do Sleep Number mattresses need special sheets?
faq.a: No, Sleep Number mattresses use standard sheet sizes, though some models with adjustable bases work best with deep-pocket sheets that can accommodate the mattress height when elevated.

faq.q: Which is cooler to sleep on, Sleep Number or Tempur-Pedic?
faq.a: Sleep Number mattresses generally sleep cooler because air chambers allow better airflow compared to Tempur-Pedic's dense memory foam, which tends to retain body heat despite cooling technologies.

faq.q: Can you flip a Tempur-Pedic or Sleep Number mattress?
faq.a: Neither mattress should be flipped as both have specific support layers designed for one-sided use, though they should be rotated 180 degrees every 3-6 months to ensure even wear.

meta_title: Sleep Number vs Tempur-Pedic (2026): Which Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Sleep Number vs Tempur-Pedic mattresses. We break down firmness adjustment, pressure relief, sleep tracking, and value to help you pick the right premium mattress.
